themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their focus on 'national priority' policies, demanding that social benefits, housing, and jobs be reserved for native citizens. This theme is particularly prominent in Spain, Italy, France, and Hungary, where parties like Vox, Fratelli d'Italia, Rassemblement National, and Tisza are framing these policies as essential to protecting national identity and economic stability.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox demands 'national priority' in Andalusia's housing and social policies, opposing EU-Mercosur trade deals and advocating for stricter immigration controls.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella emphasize 'national priority' in economic policies, criticizing EU regulations and advocating for tax cuts to protect French purchasing power.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni's government faces criticism for prioritizing national interests in housing and labor policies, while Lega pushes for stricter immigration controls.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Tisza Party proposes suspending foreign worker inflows and prioritizing Hungarian citizens in economic policies, echoing Vox's rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK campaigns on 'Britain First' policies, demanding stricter immigration controls and prioritizing native citizens in social benefits.

themes.title: Trump’s Iran war dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Right-wing media in the U.S., UK, France, Italy, and Spain are heavily focused on Trump's military actions against Iran, framing it as a decisive move to protect national security. This theme has sparked debates about U.S. troop withdrawals from Europe, rising fuel prices, and the broader geopolitical implications of the conflict.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's administration rejects Iran's peace proposals, escalates military actions, and faces criticism for bypassing congressional approval.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK supports Trump's stance on Iran, criticizing European allies for insufficient support and advocating for stronger military actions.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella criticize U.S. policies but emphasize the need for France to prioritize its national security amid rising tensions.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni's government cautiously supports U.S. actions but faces opposition criticism for not taking a stronger stance on defense spending.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox aligns with Trump's hardline stance on Iran, criticizing Spain's government for not supporting U.S. policies strongly enough.

themes.title: Meloni’s government longevity sparks cross-national debate
themes.blurb: Giorgia Meloni's government has become Italy's second-longest-serving post-war administration, sparking debates across Europe about the stability and effectiveness of right-wing leadership. This theme resonates in Germany, Hungary, France, and Poland, where far-right parties are using Meloni's success to bolster their own political ambitions.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni's government reaches 1,288 days in office, becoming Italy's second-longest-serving, with debates focusing on economic policies and reforms.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD highlights Meloni's longevity as a model for stable right-wing governance, criticizing Germany's coalition government for instability.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Tisza Party contrasts its electoral victory with Fidesz's decline, framing itself as a new force for stability in Hungary.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella use Meloni's success to argue for the viability of far-right governance in France, positioning themselves for the 2027 presidential election.

themes.title: Far-right surges in European polls and elections
themes.blurb: Far-right parties are gaining ground in polls and elections across Europe, with notable surges in Germany, France, Sweden, Norway, and the UK. This theme highlights the growing influence of parties like AfD, Rassemblement National, Sverigedemokraterna, Fremskrittspartiet, and Reform UK, which are reshaping the political landscape.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads German polls with 28% support, ahead of CDU/CSU, amid debates about its extremist ties and economic policies.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella lead polls for the 2027 French presidential election, positioning RN as a credible alternative to Macron's government.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Sverigedemokraterna disrupts Sweden's parliamentary vote-counting system, demanding stricter immigration policies and challenging the coalition government.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP leads Norwegian polls with 28.5% support, positioning itself as a potential governing party in the 2029 elections.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK gains ground in UK local elections, challenging the Conservative Party and positioning itself as the dominant right-wing force.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Far-right rhetoric is increasingly linked to political violence, with incidents reported in the U.S., UK, Italy, Sweden, and Spain. This theme highlights the growing polarization and the risks associated with inflammatory language used by far-right leaders and their supporters.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's rhetoric is linked to threats and violence against political opponents, with incidents reported at rallies and protests.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK candidates face allegations of inciting violence, with reports of assaults and threats during campaign events.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Far-right supporters in Italy clash with protesters, with incidents of violence reported at political rallies and events.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Far-right marchers in Sweden assault counter-protesters, sparking debates about the rise of political violence.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox's inflammatory rhetoric is linked to threats and harassment against political opponents, with reports of violence at campaign events.
